_meta.purpose: Sprint 4 task 4.2 cron — daily 03:30 UTC stale-alert auto-close (>30d open → resolved with metadata.auto_closed=true). Missed run ⇒ inbox drift; very-high closed_count ⇒ deeper signal that triage isn't happening.
fields_emitted: closedCount (int — number of stale alerts closed this run)
_meta.import_to: Datadog Dashboards → New → Import dashboard JSON

title: Uelfy — ALERTS_AUTO_CLOSE_RUN
description: Daily stale-alert auto-close cron. Expected: 1 event/day @ 03:30 UTC. closedCount > 0 means clinicians are not triaging within 30d.

monitors._purpose: Missed run — same blast radius as RETENTION_RUN missed (cron infra failure).
monitors.name: Uelfy — ALERTS_AUTO_CLOSE_RUN missed
monitors.message:
No ALERTS_AUTO_CLOSE_RUN in last 28 hours. The cron should fire daily at 03:30 UTC.

Runbook: docs/27-INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md §11.4

monitors._purpose: High auto-close volume — clinicians not triaging the inbox.
monitors.name: Uelfy — alerts triage backlog (high auto-close count)
monitors.message:
More than 100 stale alerts auto-closed in last 7 days. Clinicians are not triaging the inbox within 30d. Coordinate with the tenant admin.

Runbook: docs/23-CLINICAL-ENGINE.md §8.1
